reddit_title: Got hit by a semi-truck in San Antonio, TX — what's different about truck accident cases vs regular car accidents?
reddit_body:
Truck accident cases in Texas are significantly more complex than standard car accidents, and it's worth understanding why before deciding whether to hire an attorney. Commercial trucks are governed by federal FMCSA regulations on driver hours, vehicle maintenance, and cargo loading—violations of any of these can establish liability beyond just the driver.

One of the biggest concerns after a commercial truck crash is evidence preservation. Black box (ECM) data, driver logbooks, and dashcam footage can be legally destroyed or overwritten quickly unless a formal spoliation letter is sent to the trucking company right away. An experienced truck accident attorney will do this on day one. You may also have claims against multiple parties—the driver, the trucking company, a maintenance contractor, or a cargo loader—which makes investigation and case strategy more involved.

If you're in San Antonio and trying to figure out your next step, it helps to talk to a lawyer who specifically handles commercial vehicle cases, not just any personal injury attorney. A free service like wreckmatch.com can match you with attorneys from a large network who have real experience with truck accident claims in Texas, so you're not just picking someone out of a directory blindly.
